title: sideroxylon obtusifolium subsp. buxifolium
intro: Sideroxylon obtusifolium subsp. buxifolium, also known as the Box-leaf Jujube, is a subspecies of the Jujube tree native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Sideroxylon obtusifolium subsp. buxifolium include reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant are often used in traditional medicine, including tea infusions, extracts, and infusions, which are consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
